summary: DHH argues that the shift from desktop to mobile computing has been a regression for developer freedom, as Apple and Google's app stores replaced the open web's free market with arbitrary gatekeeping. He draws parallels between app store control and pre-internet walled gardens like AOL, contending that most people simply don't know what they're missing. He advocates for Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) as the best route around app store monopolies, pointing to 37signals' ONCE product as proof the approach can work. He calls on developers to collectively push PWA technology to close the remaining fidelity gap with native apps, making it his main objective for Rails 8.
key_points: App stores replaced the free market's wisdom with arbitrary bureaucratic gatekeeping, making store approval riskier than building something people want
Most mobile users have never experienced an open software market on their phones, making them easy to frighten with security stories that justify walled gardens
PWA technology is rapidly improving and represents the best practical route around app store monopolies, even if regulatory enforcement falls short
37signals' ONCE product is built entirely around a PWA strategy with no native apps, proving the approach is viable today
The current state of PWAs is like early games on a new console — developers haven't yet maximized what the platform can already do
money_quotes: The most risky part of running a software business is no longer just in making something people want, but making something the app store bureaucrats won't reject.
The wisdom of the market has been supplanted by the idiocy of the reviewer.
Comparing the kindness of kings is myopic in contrast to the distinction between free markets and even the fairest sovereign.
The rules of commerce aren't even rules, they're "guidelines", and they're as vague as they are shifty. A fig leaf covering absolute power, wielded with absolute impunity.
Imagine if your access to the Internet was governed by the same principle. If you could only visit sites pre-approved by AOL or Yahoo?
The internet treats gatekeepers as a bug and finds a way around them. Whether it be censorship or monopolies.
Don't tell me we've maximized what's doable with HTML, JavaScript, and CSS on chips ten orders of magnitude faster in 2024.
key_insight: The open web and PWAs represent developers' best path to reclaiming the freedom that app store gatekeepers took away when computing moved to the pocket.
